response.overview: Children of Despair is a haunting documentary that follows the lives of young boys growing up in regions shaped by decades of conflict, isolation, and survival. Through intimate portraits and raw observational storytelling, the film explores how violence, poverty, and inherited war culture shape childhood long before adulthood begins.Centered around a young boy carrying a rifle across barren landscapes, the documentary captures the fragile line between innocence and survival. Rather than glorifying conflict, the film examines the emotional weight carried by a generation born into instability children forced to navigate identity, fear, responsibility, and resilience in environments where childhood fades too quickly.
response.title: Children of Despair
